summary: 本轮检索到 1 项新的高相关工作：`Imagining the Sense of Touch: Touch-Informed Manipulation via Imagined Tactile Representations`。它把“想象触觉”用于接触丰富的灵巧操作，在不依赖真实触觉硬件的前提下，利用视觉和本体感觉生成触觉表征，并在仿真与真实机器人任务上验证了收益。
summary_en: This run found 1 newly relevant item: `Imagining the Sense of Touch: Touch-Informed Manipulation via Imagined Tactile Representations`. It applies imagined tactile representations to contact-rich dexterous manipulation, generating tactile cues from vision and proprioception without physical tactile hardware, and validates the approach in simulation and real-world robot tasks.

items.title: Imagining the Sense of Touch: Touch-Informed Manipulation via Imagined Tactile Representations
items.authors_or_org: Zhiyuan Zhang, Adeesh Desai, Jyun-Chi Hu, Yosuke Saka, Quan Khanh Luu, Jiuzhou Lei, Davood Soleymanzadeh, Bihao Zhang, Minghui Zheng, Yu She
items.relevance: 这项工作以接触丰富灵巧操作为核心，直接研究在没有真实触觉传感器时如何利用想象触觉提升操作性能，符合本追踪器的严格收录标准。
items.relevance_en: This work is centered on contact-rich dexterous manipulation and directly studies how imagined tactile representations can improve performance without physical tactile sensors, matching the tracker’s strict inclusion bar.
items.novelty_signal: 这是 7 月 2 日公开的全新 arXiv 论文，并明确提供项目页；它不是旧工作的重复转载，而是提出了 TacImag 这一新的触觉想象框架，并系统比较不同触觉表征对下游操作的影响。
items.novelty_signal_en: This is a newly posted arXiv paper from July 2 with an explicit project page; it is not a repost, but a new TacImag tactile-imagination framework with a systematic study of how different imagined tactile representations affect downstream manipulation.
items.analysis_zh: 该工作主要提出 TacImag，一种触觉想象框架，用视觉与本体感觉预测触觉观测，再把生成的触觉信号用于指导操作策略。它解决的是触觉硬件脆弱、标定复杂、维护成本高而又难以在部署时保留的问题，因此试图回答“机器人能否在不安装触觉传感器的情况下受益于触觉知识”。方法上，作者基于成对的视触示教训练冻结的想象模型，并在第二阶段用想象出的力场或触觉图像训练策略，部署时只依赖视觉和本体感觉在线生成触觉表征。实验覆盖 6 个仿真任务和 4 个真实世界任务；公开结果显示，真实实验中想象力场在接触敏感任务上平均提升 44.4%，想象触觉图像在纹理敏感任务上平均提升 23.3%，说明不同触觉表征与任务需求的匹配关系很关键。总体而言，这项工作把触觉从“必须由硬件测得”转化为“可被学习和合成的操作先验”；限制是公共材料尚未完整披露全部任务配置、失败案例与对更复杂触觉传感器的外推边界。
items.analysis_en: This paper introduces TacImag, a tactile-imagination framework that predicts tactile observations from vision and proprioception and then uses those synthesized signals to guide manipulation policies. It addresses a concrete deployment problem in dexterous manipulation: tactile hardware is often fragile, calibration-heavy, and costly to maintain, so the authors ask whether robots can still benefit from tactile knowledge without physical tactile sensors at test time. Methodologically, the system is trained from paired visuotactile demonstrations in a two-stage pipeline, with a frozen imagination model generating either force-field or tactile-image representations that are then consumed by the policy; at deployment, the robot relies only on vision and proprioception while generating tactile representations online. The experiments span six simulation tasks and four real-world tasks, and the reported real-world gains are 44.4% on average for contact-sensitive tasks with imagined force fields and 23.3% on average for texture-sensitive tasks with imagined tactile images, indicating that the representation choice matters. Overall, the work reframes tactile sensing as a learnable prior rather than a required piece of hardware. The main limitation in the public material is that the full task setup, failure cases, and the boundaries of generalization to other tactile modalities are not yet fully disclosed.

caveats: 本轮重点检查了 2026-07-04 之后公开的 arXiv、项目页与相关官方页面，只保留灵巧手或灵巧操作为核心贡献的条目。
未发现需要重报的重大更新项；本轮唯一新增条目来自 2026-07-02 公开的 TacImag。
